Marcela Espinosa
Design Systems Platform Redesign Cross-team Collaboration

Constellation

Applying a new design system across Drata's platform — pressure-testing patterns against real enterprise workflows and raising the quality bar for 8,000+ customers.

Senior Product Designer — one of four selected
Platform-wide design system application
Overview

A platform-wide redesign that turned a new design system into a proven one.

Constellation was a platform-wide redesign initiative that applied Drata's newly developed design system across the product.

The design system introduced new visual foundations, components, layout patterns, and interaction models intended to modernize the platform and unify experiences across product areas.

A small group of designers was tasked with applying the system across the application, pressure-testing the new patterns in real workflows, and identifying gaps or inconsistencies that required refinement before full engineering implementation.

The goal of Constellation was to create a cohesive, scalable product experience while raising the overall quality and accessibility standards of the platform.

My Role

One of four designers selected to apply and stress-test the system at scale.

I was one of four designers selected to apply the new design system across the platform.

My responsibilities included:

  • Redesigning multiple product areas using the new visual and interaction patterns
  • Identifying inconsistencies in the legacy interface and aligning workflows with the new system
  • Highlighting areas where components or patterns required refinement when applied to real workflows
  • Collaborating with the design system team to ensure components and interaction models supported complex product scenarios
  • Preparing implementation documentation and supporting engineering teams during implementation and UAT
Impact

A unified platform experience — and a design system proven at enterprise scale.

Constellation delivered a cohesive, system-wide redesign across Drata's platform, creating a unified experience under a consistent design system that significantly reduced accumulated design and technical debt across the product.

Key outcomes:

System applied consistently

Redesigned major platform areas and applied the new system consistently across layout, navigation, components, and interaction states

Legacy debt resolved

Resolved legacy inconsistencies in layout, navigation, and component usage that had built up over multiple product cycles

Quality bar raised

Raised the quality and accessibility bar across the product — establishing a higher standard that became the baseline for all future design work

System strengthened

Pressure-tested the design system against real enterprise workflows, surfacing gaps that strengthened the system before full engineering rollout

8,000+ customers

Enabled the rollout of a unified experience now used by 8,000+ Drata customers

Applying the Design System

Bringing the new system to life across every layer of the product.

Our team worked through the platform and redesigned screens using the new visual foundations and interaction patterns.

This included applying:

  • Updated layout structures
  • Standardized navigation models
  • Redesigned components and interaction states
  • Updated spacing and token systems

Applying the system across existing workflows also revealed areas where the legacy interface had accumulated design debt or inconsistent patterns that needed to be resolved.

Identifying Gaps in the System

Real workflows surface what controlled environments can't.

Applying the design system across real product workflows revealed situations where components or patterns needed refinement.

Examples included:

  • Components requiring additional states or flexibility
  • Layout patterns that needed adjustment for complex workflows
  • Navigation patterns that needed clarification when applied across different areas of the platform

These findings were shared with the designers responsible for the design system to help refine and strengthen the system as it evolved.

Cross-Team Collaboration

Consistency at scale requires constant communication.

Constellation required close collaboration between design, product, and engineering teams.

The four designers working on the initiative regularly conducted critiques and design reviews to maintain consistency across the platform.

We also worked closely with engineering teams to translate redesigned workflows into implementation-ready documentation and supported UAT to ensure the final product matched the intended design.

Reflection

Design systems only prove their value when tested against real complexity.

Constellation demonstrated how design systems must be validated against real product workflows in order to succeed at scale. Working across the platform — rather than in isolation — was what made this initiative meaningful. We weren't just applying a system; we were stress-testing it, and the gaps we found made it stronger for every team that came after us.

Applying the system across the platform helped identify edge cases and inconsistencies that could not have been discovered in isolation.

The process ensured that the design system was not only visually cohesive but also capable of supporting the complexity of Drata's enterprise compliance workflows.
